name: Unnamed village south of Millhaven
region: Northern Lordaeron, two days south of Millhaven on the road bending southwest toward Andorhal

sensoryProfile.sight: Working cottages, bakehouse smoke, carts, pump, children still on their feet with fever, cots jammed into a hall never meant for an infirmary, grain dust filming gloves and wood, and a granary full of sacks, ledgers, and one shattered clay vessel.
sensoryProfile.sound: Market noise threaded with coughs, the baker's board knocking loaves free, frightened room-quiet in the sick hall, then the hard shifts of command once the granary becomes a crime scene.
sensoryProfile.smell: Bread smoke, wet earth, rush mats, stale beer, boiled cloth, dry grain, old wood, and under all of it the sweet wrongness that is stronger in the hall and strongest in the granary spill.
sensoryProfile.touch: Mud in the square, fever heat on bodies, rough trestles and cots, frost sheeting over oil-slick boards, and the dry pressure of stored grain behind barred doors.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Ordinary life still trying to keep its shape while the plague is already inside its most trusted routines.

establishedFacts.fact: This settlement is still functioning when the party arrives; unlike Millhaven, it has not yet tipped into open collapse.
The worst cases have been moved into Dane Hall, a hall repurposed as a makeshift infirmary.
Bread and common flour from the recent grain stock are the practical infection route inside the village.
The granary contains treated stock, recent ledgers, and a live village-level watcher guarding the route.
Local households that rely less on bakery flour or keep separate oats show lighter cases.

eventsHere.event: Jaina proves from living patients that the contamination clusters gut-first and is being taken in through bread and flour.
Sylvanas matches that diagnosis with a new-supplier pattern built from prices, delivery memory, and local witness testimony.
The granary raid reveals concentrated treated liquid, Tomas the watcher, and the first hard southwest route evidence toward Andorhal.
After Tomas dies by concealed poison, Jaina and Sylvanas leave containment orders behind and ride on.
